frontier.hypothesis: The masked-2 residual mechanism has been closed at the block-local structural axis across 5 arg5_addr two-SET C-realizations plus every other block-local structural axis measured s3/s4/s5. The only remaining STRUCTURAL angle not yet touched is UPSTREAM of the debug_printf block — fn-body-scope moves of the tbl_125c/idx_1494/idx_1495/D_800F19C0 initializers at src/system.c:404-408, which sit in the same TU-level RA web (WIP notes.md: 'file-level s-reg ref-balance coupling'). If any of these fn-body-scope initializer positions is load-bearing for p107's LAUNCH signature, moving them within the loop body might shift the qty priorities without regressing the s-reg web.
frontier.mechanism: The prologue s-reg saves at src/system.c:388-408 initialize tbl_125c/idx_1494/idx_1495/D_800F19BC/D_800F19C0 outside the loop. Their C statement positions influence LUID at expand, which via qty_compare feeds birth-order tiebreaks at local-alloc. Moving one of these into the loop body (or into the do_timeout block) creates a fresh RA seat for the s-reg that could re-time the arg5-chain's LAUNCH via different birth-order tiebreaks. Risk high: WIP + s8/s9 measurements confirm the s-reg web is currently load-bearing (honest idx_1495 +13, marionation full-basin transplant +18, arg4-named +11).
frontier.next_probe: Move `D_800F19C0 = &D_80016240;` from src/system.c:408 into the do_timeout block just before the debug_printf inline block (making its store an intra-block SET rather than a prologue-emitted one). Measure sandbox --disable all. Diagnostic: does the s-reg web absorb the shift, or does the loop repeat the store per-iteration causing byte regression? If former, potential ref-lift for pp; if latter, KILLED cleanly.

frontier.hypothesis: The g3 basin's residual v1/a0 register exchange (masked 6, order-perfect per WIP notes.md:26-29) is untouched by the h5-basin structural closure. A modality-switch to g3 base and attacking arg5-qty pri>=t0 via a block-scope multi-set VALUE carrier (not address) could flip the exchange.
frontier.mechanism: In g3 basin, t0 chain is in-place ($a0), arg5 chain via v0 -> lw v1. Target requires arg5 to allocate v1 BEFORE t0 (which then falls to $a0). WIP notes.md:34-41 specifies arg5-qty needs pri>=5000 via 3refs/span6 tie + birth-order win. Unmeasured: multi-set arg5 VALUE-staging keeping the lw-dest split (see s5 F1a/F1b which measured VALUE carriers under h5 base — never tested on g3 base).
frontier.next_probe: Adopt the g3-basin base (`v0 <<= 2; arg5 = *(s32*)(v0 + (s32)tbl); t0 <<= 2; ... *(s32*)(t0 + (s32)tbl_125c) in-call`) then apply s5 F1a-style block-scope multi-set VALUE carrier for arg5: `s32 v_arg5; v_arg5 = *idx_1495; v_arg5 = *(s32*)(v0 + tbl); ... call(..., v_arg5)`. Measure sandbox --disable all. Predicted: g3 base is masked=6; probe either lifts arg5-qty pri >= t0 (flips exchange, potential closure toward 0) or is INERT (multi-set carrier equally masked-inert on g3 base). Cross-basin modality — synthesis-adjacent structural probe.

frontier.hypothesis: The decomp.me corpus scrape via tools/decomp_me_scrape.py against asm/funcs/cpu_side_move_dir_4.s may surface a structurally distinct C form from the BB2 toolchain class not present in the current search space.
frontier.mechanism: Coarse asm-overlap pre-filter; manual inspection of top hits could reveal an original-source shape our search space missed. Blocked s9/s11/s12: `python3 tools/decomp_me_scrape.py --help` fails with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'curl_cffi'.
frontier.next_probe: Operator installs curl_cffi in .venv (`source .venv/bin/activate && pip install curl_cffi`). Then next rederive session runs `python3 tools/decomp_me_scrape.py search --asm-file asm/funcs/cpu_side_move_dir_4.s` and manually inspects top asm-overlap hits for a structurally novel closing shape.
